summarization.a: Mn K-edge XANES spectra of MnO₂, 10cLi–MnO₂, and 50cLi–MnO₂ measured in TEY mode, showing a systematic shift of the absorption edge to lower energy with increasing lithium insertion, indicating partial reduction of Mn.
summarization.b: Fourier-transformed Mn K-edge EXAFS spectra of the same samples, revealing Mn–O, Mn–Mn₁, and Mn–Mn₂ coordination peaks that remain comparable to the β-MnO₂ reference, confirming preservation of the local MnO₂ structure upon lithiation.
summarization.c: Mn L-edge XANES spectra measured at a 45° incident angle, highlighting increased absorption near 641 eV for lithiated samples, which reflects the increasing contribution of Mn³⁺ states in the bulk of the films.

d.question: How do the combined Mn K-edge and L-edge XANES results support the structural stability of β-MnO₂ upon lithium insertion?
d.answer: The Mn K-edge XANES shows a systematic shift of the absorption edge to lower energy with lithium insertion, confirming manganese reduction, while the preservation of pre-edge features indicates minimal distortion of the local coordination environment. The FT EXAFS spectra further demonstrate that the Mn–O, Mn–Mn₁, and Mn–Mn₂ coordination peaks remain comparable to the β-MnO₂ reference. In addition, the Mn L-edge XANES measured at a bulk-sensitive incident angle reveals increased Mn³⁺ contributions without loss of structural features. Together, these observations indicate that lithium insertion alters the manganese oxidation state while maintaining the fundamental β-MnO₂ framework.

c.question: Which local atomic interactions are identified in the FT Mn K-edge EXAFS spectra of the samples?
c.answer: Mn–O coordination, Mn–Mn₁ interactions between edge-sharing octahedra, Mn–Mn₂ interactions between corner-sharing octahedra

a.question: Does lithium insertion lead to an immediate reduction of manganese as observed in the Mn K-edge XANES spectra?

b.question: At approximately which energy does increased absorption appear in the Mn L-edge XANES spectra for lithiated samples, indicating manganese reduction?
